# AUTO-GENERATED by split_acceptance_tests.py
# Each original test function was split into one function per top-level
# assertion so pytest reports per-check pass/fail counts.
import re

def test_gitpackage_incorporate_comment_reflects_and_semantics__a1():
    source = open('/workspace/core/dbt/task/deps.py').read()
    assert '# if one is False, make both be False.' in source, "Expected GitPackage.incorporate() to document the intended warn_unpinned merge behavior as AND semantics ('if one is False, make both be False.'). The prior comment ('if one is True, make both be True.') contradicts the actual logic and should be corrected."

def test_gitpackage_incorporate_comment_reflects_and_semantics__a2():
    source = open('/workspace/core/dbt/task/deps.py').read()
    assert re.search('def incorporate\\(self, other\\):[\\s\\S]*?warn_unpinned\\s*=\\s*self\\.warn_unpinned\\s*and\\s*other\\.warn_unpinned', source), 'Expected GitPackage.incorporate() to compute warn_unpinned using AND (self.warn_unpinned and other.warn_unpinned), so that a single False disables warnings for the merged dependency.'
